rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for interface behavior and implementation annexes for binding assignments.
Use only visible identifiers from the target component's scope when generating AGREE code.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features at that scope.
Implementation contracts must use valid output features or declared auxiliary variables for assignments.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in the correct AADL scope to preserve architecture integrity.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must target valid output features or auxiliary variables.
Guarantees in component type scope should relate visible inputs to visible outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths in component type contracts.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not found in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator diagnostics
